headline: Netflix searches for franchises after losing out on Harry Potter
summary: After losing its bid for Warner Bros Discovery’s rich trove of characters and stories, Netflix is forging ahead with the challenging work of building culture-defining franchises on its own.  Chief Creative Officer Bela Bajaria said Netflix would keep investing in original ideas, and partner with established studios like MGM and Warner Bros, to try and produce movies and series that live on for years, in the vein ‌of “Stranger Things,” “Wednesday” and “Bridgerton.”  Yet the failed attempt to buy Warner Bros' storied movie studio and HBO highlighted a vulnerability for the relative Hollywood upstart, whose catalog of original films and ‌series spans around a dozen years, compared with more than a century's worth of stories and characters for Warner Bros, Walt Disney and Universal Pictures.

headline: Netflix’s US$42.2b Warner Bros. Deal Tests Growth And Discipline
summary: Netflix (NasdaqGS:NFLX) announces a US$42.2b all-cash agreement to acquire Warner Bros. The deal would combine Netflix’s streaming platform with Warner Bros.’s film, TV, sports, and gaming assets. The acquisition expands Netflix’s reach into live sports and gaming content. For Netflix, which built its business on subscription streaming, a move of this size reshapes its role in entertainment. Warner Bros. brings a large catalog of film and TV content, as well as exposure to live sports and...
